one_liner: A gritty independent record label website with riso-print textures, oversized brutalist headlines, neon accent inks on newsprint cream, artist roster grid, release catalog with vinyl mockups, tour date list, merch shop, label history zine page, and a sticky audio player bar.
site_goal: Position Xerox Pressing Co. as a fiercely independent riso-print record label, drive vinyl pre-orders, surface upcoming tour dates, and convert listeners into fans of specific roster artists through a zine-style browsing experience anchored by a persistent audio player bar.

metadata.palette: newsprint cream, riso pink, electric cyan, black

motifs_implemented: newsprint paper grain via layered radial gradients
riso pink + cyan misregistered overlays via mix-blend-mode multiply
halftone screen overlay over body and portraits
marker scrawl annotations on featured release
vinyl-with-sleeve CSS mockups with cyan/pink/marble center labels
torn-paper press clippings with tape strips
typewriter monospace blocks for booking + demo policy
sold-out rubber-stamp motif on tour rows

sections: masthead with cyan-over-pink smudged label name + 'Est. 2014 — Brooklyn / Glasgow' tag
oversized misregistered lead headline (Loud. Independent. We press our own records.) running off the right edge
featured release card: vinyl-out-of-sleeve XRX-047 Sable Circuit 'Concrete Lullaby' with pre-order pill
three news/zine teaser cards with date stamps and marker underlines
label sampler tracklist (10 tracks, click to load into player) with oversized track numbers
tear-off newsletter strip with scissor dashed line
sticky audio player bar
'The Roster' headline with cyan strike-through
genre filter chips (all / noise / post-punk / ambient / dub / no-wave / electronic)
16 asymmetric trading-card artist cards with halftone portraits, hometowns, latest-release thumbnails, genre tags, and 'hear them' buttons that load tracks into the player
typewriter-styled demo/submissions footer
huge halftone hero portrait with riso pink + cyan blots and stencil-overlay artist name (Sable Circuit)
metadata strip: hometown, formed, members, tags, on-Xerox-since
three-column biography with footnote markers and two pink/cyan-bordered pull-quote callouts
discography rail of vinyl mockups across five releases
three torn-paper press clippings with masking-tape strips
artist's upcoming tour dates with sold-out stamps
booking + press contact block on black
left rail catalog-number index (XRX-047 → XRX-036, with stub for the rest)
filter row: format select, year select, in-stock toggle
grid of 12 flippable release cards — each shows vinyl-sticking-out-of-sleeve with cyan/pink/marble variant swatches; click or 'flip ↻' to reveal B-side tracklist + liner note excerpt
stamped pre-order banner for XRX-048
release-hero: large vinyl-and-sleeve mockup for XRX-039 Riso Twins 'Pink Static'
metadata column with artist, catalog, release date, format, pressing, mastering
pressing variant selector (black / cyan splatter /250 / pink marble /150 / test press [sold out])
buy block with price + add-to-cart pill
side A / side B tracklist with per-track play cue buttons
credits ledger (performed by, produced by, engineered, mastered, plates, sleeve art, photography, lacquers, pressed at)
multi-column liner notes
'Also from Riso Twins' rail of prior releases
'On The Road' header with riso-misregistered subtitle
filter chip bar by region (US East / US West / UK / EU) + 'hide sold-out' toggle
monospace typewriter-style table with 26 dated tour rows (date | city | venue | artist | support | tickets) and rotated sold-out rubber stamps
poster gallery of 6 tilted, taped tour posters
promoter contact block on black with typewriter brief
category sidebar (Shirts / Hoodies / Totes / Posters / Pins / Bundles) with sticky shipping note
bundle promo banner (LP + Tee + Zine $52 with rotated SAVES $11 stamp)
contact-sheet grid of 18 products across six categories with frame numbers (FR-001 → FR-018), 'add to bag' pills that increment the header cart counter
typewriter shipping policy bar at the bottom
Chapter 01 — 2014: a basement and a 4-track
Chapter 02 — 2017: the first sold-out pressing
Chapter 03 — 2020: pressing plants and pandemics
Chapter 04 — 2023: the Glasgow annex
Each chapter is a 3-column zine spread with stenciled chapter numbers, photo blocks with halftone treatment, pink/cyan pull-quote callouts, asterisk-referenced footnotes, and inkjet-streak dividers
Annotated horizontal catalog timeline (XRX-001 → XRX-047) with pink/cyan highlight cells on milestone records
Colophon: who runs the label, mailing addresses (Brooklyn + Glasgow), demo policy, press names (Doris + Audrey)

global_components.header: fixed-top masthead + brutalist nav with active state + cyan cart pill (counter updates from localStorage on add-to-cart)
global_components.footer: ink-black footer with four columns (label blurb, browse, contact, follow) + colophon line
global_components.sticky_player: ink-black bottom bar with cover, 'Now Spinning' label + marquee, prev/play/next controls, gradient scrubber (clickable + auto-progressing), volume bar, queue popout button revealing the full 10-track label sampler

interactions_implemented: sticky audio player with play/pause toggle, prev/next, click-to-seek scrubber, auto-progress simulation, marquee, and queue popout
queue popout (click Q) lists all 10 sampler tracks; click any to load
click sampler row on homepage to load that track into the player
'Hear them' buttons on roster cards load that artist's track into the player
roster genre filter chips show/hide artist cards by tag
catalog format / year / in-stock filters update visible release cards
release-card click or 'flip ↻' button toggles 3D card flip to reveal B-side
tour region filter chips + 'hide sold-out' checkbox
release-detail variant swatch selector (toggles .on state, OOS variants disabled)
add-to-cart buttons increment a localStorage cart counter rendered in the header pill
newsletter forms intercept submit and confirm with a 'Stamped.' button state
mobile_behavior: below 980px the sticky player collapses to cover + marquee + 3 controls; nav wraps; roster, catalog, merch, and shop layouts stack into a single column; bio + liner notes + zine body collapse from 3-col to 1-col; tour rows hide venue + support; pull-quotes still register pink/cyan

tech.stack: vanilla HTML5 + CSS3 + a single vanilla JS file
tech.imagery_strategy: all artwork is generated in CSS — vinyl mockups via layered radial-gradient + box-shadow, sleeve art via gradient + dot-pattern fills, halftone portraits via radial silhouette + dot-screen overlays, riso textures via mix-blend-mode multiply layers, masking tape via solid yellow blocks with dashed borders
